info.name: Luke
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to Luke

1:
Many have undertaken to draw up an account of the things that have been fulfilled
 among us,
Introduction
1:1 Or been surely believed
2: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of the word.
Introduction
3: With this in mind, since I myself have carefully investigated everything from the beginning, I too decided to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
Introduction
4: so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
5: In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah; his wife Elizabeth was also a descendant of Aaron.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
6: Both of them were righteous in the sight of God, observing all the Lord’s commands and decrees blamelessly.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
7: But they were childless because Elizabeth was not able to conceive, and they were both very old.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
8: Once when Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
9: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
10: And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
11: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
12: When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
13: But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to call him John.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
14: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth,
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
15: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
16: He will bring back many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
17: And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the parents to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
18: Zechariah asked the angel, “How can I be sure of this? I am an old man and my wife is well along in years.”
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
19: The angel said to him, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to tell you this good news.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
20: And now you will be silent and not able to speak until the day this happens, because you did not believe my words, which will come true at their appointed time.”
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
21: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he stayed so long in the temple.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
22: When he came out, he could not speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he kept making signs to them but remained unable to speak.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
23: When his time of service was completed, he returned home.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
24: After this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion.
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
25: “The Lord has done this for me,” she said. “In these days he has shown his favor and taken away my disgrace among the people.”
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
26: In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a town in Galilee,
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
27: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
28: The angel went to her and said, “Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you.”
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
29: Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
30: But the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary; you have found favor with God.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
31: You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you are to call him Jesus.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
32: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David,
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
33: and he will reign over Jacob’s descendants forever; his kingdom will never end.”
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
34: “How will this be,” Mary asked the angel, “since I am a virgin?”
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
35:
The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the holy one to be born will be called
 the Son of God.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
1:35 Or So the child to be born will be called holy,
36: Even Elizabeth your relative is going to have a child in her old age, and she who was said to be unable to conceive is in her sixth month.
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
37: For no word from God will ever fail.”
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
38: “I am the Lord’s servant,” Mary answered. “May your word to me be fulfilled.” Then the angel left her.
Mary Visits Elizabeth
39: At that time Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea,
Mary Visits Elizabeth
40: where she entered Zechariah’s home and greeted Elizabeth.
Mary Visits Elizabeth
41: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
Mary Visits Elizabeth
42: In a loud voice she exclaimed: “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!
Mary Visits Elizabeth
43: But why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
Mary Visits Elizabeth
44: As soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
Mary Visits Elizabeth
45: Blessed is she who has believed that the Lord would fulfill his promises to her!”
Mary’s Song
46:
And Mary said:
“My soul glorifies the Lord
Mary’s Song
47: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
Mary’s Song
48:
for he has been mindful
of the humble state of his servant.
From now on all generations will call me blessed,
Mary’s Song
49:
for the Mighty One has done great things for me—
holy is his name.
Mary’s Song
50:
His mercy extends to those who fear him,
from generation to generation.
Mary’s Song
51:
He has performed mighty deeds with his arm;
he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts.
52:
He has brought down rulers from their thrones
but has lifted up the humble.
53:
He has filled the hungry with good things
but has sent the rich away empty.
54:
He has helped his servant Israel,
remembering to be merciful
55:
to Abraham and his descendants forever,
just as he promised our ancestors.”
56: Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.
The Birth of John the Baptist
57: When it was time for Elizabeth to have her baby, she gave birth to a son.
The Birth of John the Baptist
58: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they shared her joy.
The Birth of John the Baptist
59: On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him after his father Zechariah,
The Birth of John the Baptist
60: but his mother spoke up and said, “No! He is to be called John.”
The Birth of John the Baptist
61: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who has that name.”
The Birth of John the Baptist
62: Then they made signs to his father, to find out what he would like to name the child.
The Birth of John the Baptist
63: He asked for a writing tablet, and to everyone’s astonishment he wrote, “His name is John.”
The Birth of John the Baptist
64: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue set free, and he began to speak, praising God.
The Birth of John the Baptist
65: All the neighbors were filled with awe, and throughout the hill country of Judea people were talking about all these things.
The Birth of John the Baptist
66: Everyone who heard this wondered about it, asking, “What then is this child going to be?” For the Lord’s hand was with him.
67: His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:
68:
“Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel,
because he has come to his people and redeemed them.
69:
He has raised up a horn
 of salvation for us
in the house of his servant David
; 1:69 Horn here symbolizes a strong king.
70: (as he said through his holy prophets of long ago),
71:
salvation from our enemies
and from the hand of all who hate us—
72:
to show mercy to our ancestors
and to remember his holy covenant,
73: the oath he swore to our father Abraham:
74:
to rescue us from the hand of our enemies,
and to enable us to serve him without fear
75: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
76:
And you, my child, will be called a prophet of the Most High;
for you will go on before the Lord to prepare the way for him,
77:
to give his people the knowledge of salvation
through the forgiveness of their sins,
78:
because of the tender mercy of our God,
by which the rising sun will come to us from heaven
79:
to shine on those living in darkness
and in the shadow of death,
to guide our feet into the path of peace.”
80:
And the child grew and became strong in spirit
; and he lived in the wilderness until he appeared publicly to Israel.

1: In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.
The Birth of Jesus
2:
(This was the first census that took place while
 Quirinius was governor of Syria.)
The Birth of Jesus
2:2 Or This census took place before
3: And everyone went to their own town to register.
The Birth of Jesus
4: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David.
The Birth of Jesus
5: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him and was expecting a child.
The Birth of Jesus
6: While they were there, the time came for the baby to be born,
The Birth of Jesus
7: and she gave birth to her firstborn, a son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no guest room available for them.
The Birth of Jesus
8: And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.
The Birth of Jesus
9: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
The Birth of Jesus
10: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people.
The Birth of Jesus
11: Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is the Messiah, the Lord.
The Birth of Jesus
12: This will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.”
The Birth of Jesus
13: Suddenly a great company of the heavenly host appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,
The Birth of Jesus
14:
“Glory to God in the highest heaven,
and on earth peace to those on whom his favor rests.”
The Birth of Jesus
15: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about.”
The Birth of Jesus
16: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby, who was lying in the manger.
The Birth of Jesus
17: When they had seen him, they spread the word concerning what had been told them about this child,
The Birth of Jesus
18: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.
The Birth of Jesus
19: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.
The Birth of Jesus
20: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had heard and seen, which were just as they had been told.
The Birth of Jesus
21: On the eighth day, when it was time to circumcise the child, he was named Jesus, the name the angel had given him before he was conceived.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
22: When the time came for the purification rites required by the Law of Moses, Joseph and Mary took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
Jesus Presented in the Temple
23:
(as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male is to be consecrated to the Lord”
),
Jesus Presented in the Temple
24: and to offer a sacrifice in keeping with what is said in the Law of the Lord: “a pair of doves or two young pigeons.”
Jesus Presented in the Temple
25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
26: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
27: Moved by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what the custom of the Law required,
Jesus Presented in the Temple
28: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying:
Jesus Presented in the Temple
29:
“Sovereign Lord, as you have promised,
you may now dismiss
 your servant in peace.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
; 2:29 Or promised, / now dismiss
30: For my eyes have seen your salvation,
Jesus Presented in the Temple
31: which you have prepared in the sight of all nations:
Jesus Presented in the Temple
32:
a light for revelation to the Gentiles,
and the glory of your people Israel.”
Jesus Presented in the Temple
33: The child’s father and mother marveled at what was said about him.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
34: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother: “This child is destined to cause the falling and rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,
Jesus Presented in the Temple
35: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
Jesus Presented in the Temple
36: There was also a prophet, Anna, the daughter of Penuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old; she had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
Jesus Presented in the Temple
37:
and then was a widow until she was eighty-four.
 She never left the temple but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
2:37 Or then had been a widow for eighty-four years.
38: Coming up to them at that very moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
39: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.
Jesus Presented in the Temple
40: And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
41: Every year Jesus’ parents went to Jerusalem for the Festival of the Passover.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
42: When he was twelve years old, they went up to the festival, according to the custom.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
43: After the festival was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
44: Thinking he was in their company, they traveled on for a day. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
45: When they did not find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
46: After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
47: Everyone who heard him was amazed at his understanding and his answers.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
48: When his parents saw him, they were astonished. His mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you.”
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
49: “Why were you searching for me?” he asked. “Didn’t you know I had to be in my Father’s house?”
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
2:49 Or be about my Father’s business
50: But they did not understand what he was saying to them.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
51: Then he went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But his mother treasured all these things in her heart.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple
52: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.
The Boy Jesus at the Temple

1: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar—when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Iturea and Traconitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene—
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
2: during the high-priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3: He went into all the country around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
4:
As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet:
“A voice of one calling in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the Lord,
make straight paths for him.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
5:
Every valley shall be filled in,
every mountain and hill made low.
The crooked roads shall become straight,
the rough ways smooth.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
6: And all people will see God’s salvation.’ ”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:6 Isaiah 40:3-5
7: John said to the crowds coming out to be baptized by him, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
8: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance. And do not begin to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
9: The ax is already at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
10: “What should we do then?” the crowd asked.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
11: John answered, “Anyone who has two shirts should share with the one who has none, and anyone who has food should do the same.”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
12: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. “Teacher,” they asked, “what should we do?”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
13: “Don’t collect any more than you are required to,” he told them.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
14:
Then some soldiers asked him, “And what should we do?”
He replied, “Don’t extort money and don’t accuse people falsely—be content with your pay.”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
15: The people were waiting expectantly and were all wondering in their hearts if John might possibly be the Messiah.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
16:
John answered them all, “I baptize you with
 water. But one who is more powerful than I will come, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with
 the Holy Spirit and fire.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:16 Or in
17: His winnowing fork is in his hand to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his barn, but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
18: And with many other words John exhorted the people and proclaimed the good news to them.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
19: But when John rebuked Herod the tetrarch because of his marriage to Herodias, his brother’s wife, and all the other evil things he had done,
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
20: Herod added this to them all: He locked John up in prison.
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
21: When all the people were being baptized, Jesus was baptized too. And as he was praying, heaven was opened
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
22: and the Holy Spirit descended on him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven: “You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.”
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
23:
Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph,
the son of Heli,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
24:
the son of Matthat,
the son of Levi, the son of Melki,
the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
25:
the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos,
the son of Nahum, the son of Esli,
the son of Naggai,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
26:
the son of Maath,
the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein,
the son of Josek, the son of Joda,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
27:
the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa,
the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel,
the son of Neri,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
28:
the son of Melki,
the son of Addi, the son of Cosam,
the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
29:
the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer,
the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat,
the son of Levi,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
30:
the son of Simeon,
the son of Judah, the son of Joseph,
the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
31:
the son of Melea, the son of Menna,
the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan,
the son of David,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
32:
the son of Jesse,
the son of Obed, the son of Boaz,
the son of Salmon,
 the son of Nahshon,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
; 3:32 Some early manuscripts Sala
33:
the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram,
the son of Hezron, the son of Perez,
the son of Judah,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
; 3:33 Some manuscripts Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Arni; other manuscripts vary widely.
34:
the son of Jacob,
the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham,
the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
35:
the son of Serug, the son of Reu,
the son of Peleg, the son of Eber,
the son of Shelah,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
36:
the son of Cainan,
the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem,
the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
37:
the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch,
the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel,
the son of Kenan,
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus
38:
the son of Enosh,
the son of Seth, the son of Adam,
the son of God.
The Baptism and Genealogy of Jesus

1: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, left the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
2:
where for forty days he was tempted
 by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and at the end of them he was hungry.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
4:2 The Greek for tempted can also mean tested.
3: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
4: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
5: The devil led him up to a high place and showed him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
6: And he said to him, “I will give you all their authority and splendor; it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
7: If you worship me, it will all be yours.”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
8: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
9: The devil led him to Jerusalem and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God,” he said, “throw yourself down from here.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
10: Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
11:
they will lift you up in their hands,
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’
”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
12: Jesus answered, “It is said: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
13: When the devil had finished all this tempting, he left him until an opportune time.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
14: Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread through the whole countryside.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
15: He was teaching in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
16: He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom. He stood up to read,
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
17: and the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. Unrolling it, he found the place where it is written:
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
18: “The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free,
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
19: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
4:19              Isaiah 61:1,2 (see Septuagint); Isaiah 58:6
20: Then he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fastened on him.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
21: He began by saying to them, “Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing.”
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
22: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his lips. “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?” they asked.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
23: Jesus said to them, “Surely you will quote this proverb to me: ‘Physician, heal yourself!’ And you will tell me, ‘Do here in your hometown what we have heard that you did in Capernaum.’ ”
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
24: “Truly I tell you,” he continued, “no prophet is accepted in his hometown.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
25: I assure you that there were many widows in Israel in Elijah’s time, when the sky was shut for three and a half years and there was a severe famine throughout the land.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
26: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them, but to a widow in Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
27: And there were many in Israel with leprosy in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed—only Naaman the Syrian.”
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
4:27 The Greek word traditionally translated leprosy was used for various diseases affecting the skin.
28: All the people in the synagogue were furious when they heard this.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
29: They got up, drove him out of the town, and took him to the brow of the hill on which the town was built, in order to throw him off the cliff.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
30: But he walked right through the crowd and went on his way.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
31: Then he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath he taught the people.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
32: They were amazed at his teaching, because his words had authority.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
33: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by a demon, an impure spirit. He cried out at the top of his voice,
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
34: “Go away! What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
35: “Be quiet!” Jesus said sternly. “Come out of him!” Then the demon threw the man down before them all and came out without injuring him.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
36: All the people were amazed and said to each other, “What words these are! With authority and power he gives orders to impure spirits and they come out!”
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
37: And the news about him spread throughout the surrounding area.
Jesus Heals Many
38: Jesus left the synagogue and went to the home of Simon. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Jesus to help her.
Jesus Heals Many
39: So he bent over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and began to wait on them.
Jesus Heals Many
40: At sunset, the people brought to Jesus all who had various kinds of sickness, and laying his hands on each one, he healed them.
Jesus Heals Many
41: Moreover, demons came out of many people, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Messiah.
Jesus Heals Many
